sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison of NVDA and AMD and TSM and ASML uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.

NVDA reports $216B of revenue (65.5% YoY) and $120B of net income in 2026-01-25. NVDA's profile is further defined by net margin of 55.6%, debt-to-equity of 0.06x, free cash flow of $96.7B.

AMD reports $34.6B of revenue (34.3% YoY) and $4.33B of net income in 2025-12-27. AMD's profile is further defined by net margin of 12.5%, debt-to-equity of 0.05x, free cash flow of $6.74B.

TSM reports $88.3B of revenue (25.0% YoY) and $35.3B of net income in 2024-12-31. TSM's profile is further defined by net margin of 40.0%, free cash flow of $26.5B.

ASML reports $38.4B of revenue (30.7% YoY) and $11.3B of net income in 2025-12-31. ASML's profile is further defined by net margin of 29.4%, debt-to-equity of 0.22x, free cash flow of $13.0B. NVDA is the larger company on reported annual revenue.

paragraphs.text: This comparison of NVDA and AMD and TSM and ASML uses each company's most recent annual filing and should be read as a comparison of financial profiles rather than as a claim that each company is economically identical.
NVDA reports $216B of revenue (65.5% YoY) and $120B of net income in 2026-01-25. NVDA's profile is further defined by net margin of 55.6%, debt-to-equity of 0.06x, free cash flow of $96.7B.
AMD reports $34.6B of revenue (34.3% YoY) and $4.33B of net income in 2025-12-27. AMD's profile is further defined by net margin of 12.5%, debt-to-equity of 0.05x, free cash flow of $6.74B.
TSM reports $88.3B of revenue (25.0% YoY) and $35.3B of net income in 2024-12-31. TSM's profile is further defined by net margin of 40.0%, free cash flow of $26.5B.

ASML reports $38.4B of revenue (30.7% YoY) and $11.3B of net income in 2025-12-31. ASML's profile is further defined by net margin of 29.4%, debt-to-equity of 0.22x, free cash flow of $13.0B. NVDA is the larger company on reported annual revenue.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
For NVDA, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Gross margin of 71.1% leaves a large share of revenue available to absorb operating costs and support profitability. ROE of 101.5% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $96.7B and debt-to-equity of 0.06x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

For AMD, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Revenue rose 34.3% year over year, marking a meaningful step-up in scale. Current ratio of 2.85x provides a wide cushion against near-term obligations. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $6.74B and debt-to-equity of 0.05x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

For TSM, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Gross margin of 56.1% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses. ROE of 29.0% points to solid earnings generation relative to book equity.

For ASML, the most favorable feature in the current figures is straightforward: Gross margin of 52.8% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses. ROE of 53.5% points to very strong earnings generation relative to book equity. That picture sits alongside free cash flow of $13.0B and debt-to-equity of 0.22x, which helps show how profitability is balanced against financing risk.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
The risk discussion is limited to what each annual filing supports directly, and missing peer metrics are left blank rather than forced into a misleading side-by-side comparison.

For NVDA, the main areas of financial pressure in the reporting period are as follows. The current period does not separately disclose Marketable Securities, even though that item appears in the prior period.

AMD does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

TSM does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

ASML does not present one dominant area of financial pressure in the reporting period used here, although that should not be mistaken for a risk-free profile.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
All peer figures are drawn from annual filings. Missing metrics remain blank, so the note should be read as a comparison of what each filing supports rather than as a fully normalized screen.

NVDA enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and at least one area that deserves caution. Gross margin of 71.1% leaves a large share of revenue available to absorb operating costs and support profitability. The current period does not separately disclose Marketable Securities, even though that item appears in the prior period.

AMD enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and no single dominant area of immediate financial stress. Revenue rose 34.3% year over year, marking a meaningful step-up in scale.

TSM enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and no single dominant area of immediate financial stress. Gross margin of 56.1% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses.

ASML enters the comparison with clear areas of support in the numbers and no single dominant area of immediate financial stress. Gross margin of 52.8% leaves the company with a reasonable buffer to absorb operating expenses.
